text.title: PostgreSQL schema patterns: entities, indexing, soft deletes, audit, partitioning
text.summary: Core schema design patterns for PostgreSQL: normalized entity relationships with constraints, access-pattern-driven indexes, partial indexes for soft deletes, audit triggers, and range partitioning.

section.title: Entity relationships with constraints

rule.statement: Every table must have: UUID or UUIDv7 primary key, NOT NULL on all required columns, UNIQUE constraints for natural keys, FK with explicit ON DELETE semantics, CHECK constraints for bounded domains (enums, ranges, non-negative quantities).
rule.rationale: Constraints at the database level cannot be bypassed by application bugs or direct SQL access. Missing FK enforcement allows orphaned rows that cause silent data corruption.

section.reference: E-commerce DDL: users, products, orders, order_items with all constraints and indexes.

rule.statement: Create indexes derived from stated query patterns only. For skewed data (status IN ('pending', 'processing') where 90% is 'delivered'), use partial indexes. For text search, use GIN with pg_trgm. For time-ordered inserts, prefer UUIDv7 over UUIDv4 to preserve B-tree locality.
rule.rationale: Over-indexing degrades INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE performance proportionally to the number of indexes. Index for the queries you have, not the queries you might have.

rule.statement: Add a nullable deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ column. Create a partial index on the unique key filtered WHERE deleted_at IS NULL. Create a view (active_X) for queries that never need deleted records. Every query on soft-deleted tables must include the WHERE filter.
rule.rationale: Without the partial index on the unique key, soft-deleted records block re-insertion of the same natural key (e.g., a user re-registering with the same email).

rule.statement: Use range partitioning by time column for append-heavy tables (events, logs, orders) where old partitions can be detached and archived. Create indexes on each partition, not just the parent. Document the partition creation and archival schedule in a runbook.
rule.rationale: Range partitioning allows pg_partman to auto-create future partitions and detach old ones. Without documented partition management, partitions grow unbounded and performance degrades.
